meta.description: NevUp Hiring Hackathon 2026 — Seed Dataset. 10 synthetic trader profiles with labelled behavioural pathologies.

traders.name: Alex Mercer
traders.description: Consistently opens trades within 90 seconds of a losing close in an anxious/fearful state. Each revenge sequence results in further losses, deepening the drawdown.

trades.entryRationale: Trend continuation setup per morning plan
Still trying to get back losses

traders.name: Jordan Lee
traders.description: Opens 12-15 trades in 25-minute windows multiple times per session. Win rate collapses during these bursts. Performance is significantly better in the calmer tail of each session.

traders.name: Sam Rivera
traders.description: Consistently waits for confirmation that a move has started, then enters late. Entry rationale repeatedly references 'catching the rest of the move'. Plan adherence is 1 on FOMO entries vs 4-5 on planned entries.

trades.entryRationale: Price already moved a lot, trying to catch the rest of the move

traders.name: Casey Kim
traders.description: Has a written trading plan but deviates from it in ~80% of trades. The correlation between plan adherence and outcome is stark — adherent trades win, non-adherent trades lose.

trades.entryRationale: Felt like a good setup but not in plan

traders.name: Morgan Bell
traders.description: Closes winning trades after only 3-12 minutes out of fear of reversal. Trades held to plan target run 40-120 minutes and hit full profit. Risk-reward ratio is severely impaired by this pattern.

trades.entryRationale: Cut early — was scared it would reverse

traders.name: Taylor Grant
traders.description: Holds losing trades for 3-7 hours hoping for a reversal. Winning trades are exited promptly. Entry rationale on losers repeatedly says 'kept hoping it would come back'. Massive asymmetry in time held vs outcome.

traders.name: Riley Stone
traders.description: Performs well in the first half of sessions (win rate ~80%) and collapses after the first loss. Session tilt index spikes to >0.7 after the first losing trade. Entry rationale shifts to 'trying to get green on the day'.

traders.name: Drew Patel
traders.description: Win rate is ~90% in trades taken before 11:30 AM and ~15% in trades taken after 1:30 PM. Continues trading into the afternoon despite consistent losses in that window.

traders.name: Quinn Torres
traders.description: Position size jumps 10-20x normal after a winning trade. These oversized trades frequently result in large losses that wipe out multiple normal-size winning trades. The pattern is driven by post-win overconfidence.

trades.entryRationale: Felt confident after last win, went bigger

traders.name: Avery Chen
traders.description: Disciplined trader with no dominant pathology. Consistent plan adherence (3-5), calm emotional state, appropriate trade frequency, and structured entry rationale. Serves as the control profile for evaluation.
